checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the textual content of the agenda, meeting minutes, resignation letter, and goals without fabricating new sentences or paragraphs. The section titles like 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' are reasonably inferred from the content (minutes from the Jan 7 meeting).

I verified all contact information provided in the resignation letter (page 5). The names, titles, and mailing addresses for Jason P. Steward (1528 Prison Camp Rd, Williamston, NC 27892) and Donna Wright (PO Box 17209, Raleigh, NC 27619) in the output match the source exactly. No phone numbers, emails, or URLs were present to audit.

All pages and major sections (Agenda, Minutes, Resignation, Goals, Dates) are represented in the JSON. While some sections are severely truncated (see C5), the sections themselves are present in the structure.

Two critical tables in the document are severely truncated. 1) The 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 Standards Committee Attendance' table on page 2 contains approximately 25 rows in the source but only 2 rows in the JSON (headers only). 2) The 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table on page 4 contains 12 rows in the source but only 1 row in the JSON. In both instances, over 90% of the data rows are missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance table (page 2) with ~25 rows of members and staff; Roll Call Votes table (page 4) with 11 member votes.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Standards Committee Meeting Attendance' table in JSON has 2 rows. The 'Roll Call Votes' table in JSON has 1 row.
checks.b_evidence_location: Standards Meeting Minutes January, elements index 5 and 13.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' section (covering pages 2-4) is missing a substantial amount of information due to the failure to extract data from the Attendance and Roll Call Votes tables. The names of attendees and the actual votes cast are entirely absent from the output, representing a loss of more than 25% of the information for those pages.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance data (names and checkmarks on page 2) and Roll Call Vote results (Y/N marks on page 4).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON output for these tables contains only the title/header strings and no member data.
checks.b_evidence_location: Standards Meeting Minutes January, elements index 5 and 13.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text extraction for the body of the minutes and the agenda items is accurate. Typographical errors in the source (e.g., 'Tine' instead of 'Tina' on page 3) were correctly preserved.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the JSON output corresponds to the source text without any unintended duplication. While certain titles and headers (e.g., 'North Carolina 911 Board') appear multiple times, this reflects their presence on different pages of the source document.

The JSON uses a flat hierarchy of H2 headings for all section titles and sub-headers. While this doesn't utilize H1 or H3 levels to show nesting, it does not skip levels or nest headings incorrectly according to the provided rules. The hierarchy is consistent and appropriate for document navigation.

Multiple tables in the document are structurally corrupted or incomplete. The 'Attendance' table on page 2 and the 'Roll Call Votes' table on page 4 in the JSON output only contain header rows. The data rows (names of members, staff, and their respective attendance or vote statuses) which are clearly visible in the source PDF images are entirely absent from the JSON structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table on Page 2 contains rows for 'Commissioner Marty Cooke', 'Greg Coltrain', etc. The table on Page 4 contains rows for 'Commissioner Marty Cooke', 'Greg Coltrain', etc. with 'Y' indicators.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'rows' array for the table in section 1, element 5 (Attendance) contains only 2 entries (headers). The 'rows' array for the table in section 1, element 9 (Roll Call Votes) contains only 1 entry (header).
checks.b_evidence_location: Standards Meeting Minutes January, section 1, elements 5 and 9

checks.a_analysis: The JSON output maintains the logical reading order of the source document, proceeding through the agenda, minutes, resignation correspondence, and committee goals/dates in the correct sequence.

The numbered items in the meeting minutes (page 3) and the committee goals (page 6) are correctly identified and structured as ordered lists in the JSON. The dates on page 7 are represented as an unordered list, which is a structurally faithful conversion of the vertical list in the source.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The metadata reports 8 headings in the semantic.json, while the rendered HTML contains 13. This is a mismatch of 5 headings (62.5%), which exceeds the allowed threshold of 3+ headings or 10%. This indicates the renderer added headings or failed to correctly translate the JSON structure.
There are no links present in the JSON, and there are no clickable URLs visible in the source document that were failed to be captured.

checks.a_analysis: There is a significant layout rearrangement on the second page of content. In the source PDF (page 2), the header information ('North Carolina 911 Board... Draft Minutes') appears at the top of the page, above the 'Meeting Attendees' line and the attendance table. In the HTML output, this header text is placed below the image of the attendance table and the 'Meeting Attendees' section. Furthermore, the content from page 4 (Roll Call Votes) is missing its entire data structure, significantly altering the visual flow and completeness of that section.
checks.b_evidence_source: The top of PDF page 2 shows 'North Carolina 911 Board Standards Committee Meeting Draft Minutes Thursday, October 7, 2021'.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML, this text block appears after the 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' image section and before the 'Standards Committee Meeting Attendance' heading.
checks.b_evidence_location: After the 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' section.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains two major table rendering failures. First, the HTML table under 'Standards Committee Meeting Attendance' contains only the header row; all data rows for committee members and staff are missing. Second, the 'Roll Call Votes' table from page 4 of the source PDF is rendered in the HTML as a text box containing only header information, with the actual data table (names and 'Y' votes) entirely absent.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF page 2 (Attendance table with rows for members like Commissioner Marty Cooke) and PDF page 4 (Roll Call table with columns and 'Y' markers).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table under 'Standards Committee Meeting Attendance' is empty except for headers. The 'Roll Call Votes' section in the HTML lacks the data table entirely.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Standards Committee Meeting Attendance' and 'Roll Call Votes'.

checks.a_analysis: Meaningful images such as the NCDIT 911 Board logo are present in the HTML output. The attendance table appears to have been captured as an image in one section, ensuring its visual data is present even if the corresponding HTML table is broken.

The attendance information from page 2 of the PDF is visually duplicated in the HTML. It appears first as an image-based block under the heading 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' and then again immediately following it as an empty HTML table shell under the heading 'Standards Committee Meeting Attendance'.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 2 contains one instance of the Attendance table.
checks.b_evidence_output: The attendance table data/headers appear twice in the HTML output within the same general area.
checks.b_evidence_location: Between 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' and 'Standards Committee Meeting Attendance'.

checks.a_analysis: The overall quality is degraded due to the broken and duplicated tables, as well as inconsistent section naming. For example, the resignation letter is prefixed with a strange, non-human-friendly heading 'standards06212021'. These issues combined with missing content (the roll call votes) result in a poor user experience.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 5 is a formal resignation letter from Jason P. Steward.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML displays this content under the heading 'standards06212021'.
